title: Implementing SOM and FLAS for Grid Sorting
description:
## Lab 4: Grid Sorting Algorithms

You will implement SOM and a simplified FLAS from scratch, apply both to sorting synthetic 3DGS attributes into a 2D grid, measure quality using GAF and DPQ metrics, and demonstrate the compression benefit on a real entropy estimate.

### Part 1: Quality Metrics
Implement the D_p path metric, GAF, and a simplified DPQ. Use them to evaluate random vs. sorted arrangements.

### Part 2: SOM Implementation
Build a SOM that sorts N RGB triples onto a 2D grid. Experiment with learning rate schedules and neighborhood radius decay. Visualize the sorted grid at multiple stages of training.

### Part 3: FLAS Implementation
Implement the core FLAS loop: Gaussian filter → local linear assignment (4×4 patches) → repeat. Compare quality and runtime to SOM on the same data.

### Part 4: 3DGS Position Sorting
Apply both algorithms to sort 1024 3D Gaussian positions (xyz) onto a 32×32 grid. Measure the entropy of the sorted vs. unsorted position arrays to quantify compression benefit.
objectives: Implement D_p path metric and Grid Adjacency Fidelity from scratch.
Build a complete SOM and tune its hyperparameters for 2D grid sorting.
Implement simplified FLAS using scipy Gaussian filter and linear_sum_assignment.
Compare SOM and FLAS on quality metrics and runtime for N=1024 and N=4096.
Demonstrate compression improvement by measuring entropy before and after sorting.
